summary: A low rectangular two-story (approx.) decorative building raised one block above ground on a stonebrick edge with three tall corner towers. Main wall material appears pale (sandstone or birch-like) with horizontal wooden trim and white corner/ledge accents; many small glass windows in regular rows. Towers are built from darker stonebrick columns with layered slab caps and red brick small roof elements visible at center. The structure floats slightly above ground level (raised platform).

shape.overall_form: rectangular main mass with three tall rectangular towers at corners/edges and a low central roof element
shape.symmetry: rough bilateral symmetry along central axis (towers mirrored left/right; center slightly asymmetric)

elements: small regular rectangular windows in rows across both floors
raised stonebrick foundation edge (1 block high) that the building sits on
three tall square towers with slab caps and overhanging layered roofs
white corner/ledge blocks forming vertical supports and a perimeter parapet
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular sandstone (or pale) two-story box approx. 20x12 with a 1-block stonebrick foundation rim around the footprint.
Add white blocks (quartz or similar) as vertical corner pillars and a 1-block-high parapet around the roof edge.
Place wooden planks as a horizontal band between the two floors on all visible sides.

Create three tall 3x3 stonebrick tower columns rising above the roof at the corners/edges; cap them with stacked stone slabs and a 3x3 overhanging slab roof with a thin red-brick top layer for the central small roofs.
Insert small glass windows in a regular grid: two rows per side, spaced by wooden band and corner pillars.
Raise the main building so its base sits one block above ground and add stonebrick underside edge visible around perimeter.
uncertainties: Exact material identities: pale wall could be smooth sandstone, regular sandstone, or birch planks; listed as sandstone with moderate confidence.
Precise internal floor count and interior layout are not visible; floors_estimate=2 is based on two external horizontal bands of windows.
Exact footprint dimensions and tower positions are approximate based on visual scale; width/depth/height are estimates.
Some decorative blocks (white trim) might be quartz, wool, or concrete; identified as quartz-ish with moderate confidence.
Small floating glowstone blocks shown separately may be scaffolding/lighting and not part of final structure.
